rationale: The citing paper does more than list this reference as background: it uses Zervas et al. (2017) to position its study within causal, financially oriented sharing-economy impact research. It also explicitly treats Zervas et al. as a methodological benchmark, noting that an aggregate analysis could be done "as in" that paper before justifying an individual-level design instead. This gives the citation a clear functional role in framing and empirical design differentiation.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the empirical line exemplified by Zervas et al. (2017) on estimating platform entry effects, while refining the analytic granularity from aggregate market outcomes to individual-level spending behavior in a new domain (e-scooters and restaurants).
